data.title: U.S. stocks churn in volatile trade after heated Trump-Zelenskyy meeting
data.description:
U.S. stocks whipsawed in Friday afternoon trade, as market participants digested a heated argument between President Donald Trump and Ukraine's leader Volodymyr Zelenskyy and its possible ramifications for geopolitics.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) fell as much as -0.3% after the developments, and was last flat. The tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was also flat, although slightly down; and the blue-chip Dow (DJI) was +0.2%.

The White House posted a video showing Trump, Zelenskyy and U.S. Vice President JD Vance speaking together at the Oval Office. The video shows the discussion become increasingly heated, with Trump raising his voice at Zelenskyy and telling him that he was "gambling" with World War Three.

The parties canceled a planned press conference, and Zelenskyy was then seen leaving the White House.

"I have determined that President Zelenskyy is not ready for Peace if America is involved, because he feels our involvement gives him a big advantage in negotiations," Trump said on his Truth Social service, owned by Trump Media & Technology Group (DJT).

"I don’t want advantage, I want PEACE. He disrespected the United States of America in its cherished Oval Office. He can come back when he is ready for Peace,” Trump added.

U.S. Treasury yields slightly added to their losses. The 10-year yield (US10Y) was now down 4 basis points to 4.23%, while the 2-year yield (US2Y) was down 6 basis points to 4.00%.

Earlier, Wall Street's major averages overcame a mixed open to briefly trade higher, as market participants received a key inflation reading that came in-line with expectations, before turning briefly red.

The Core PCE Price Index - widely seen as the Federal Reserve's preferred inflation gauge - came in at +0.3% M/M in January vs. +0.3% expected and +0.2% prior, and +2.6% Y/Y vs. +2.6% consensus and +2.9% prior (revised from +2.8%).

The broader PCE Price Index, which includes food and energy prices, was +0.3% M/M vs. +0.4% consensus and +0.3% prior, and +2.5% Y/Y vs. +2.5% consensus and +2.6% prior.

"Inflation is still a challenge not just for the Fed but for consumers looking to spend on discretionary items. Earlier this month we learned the CPI surprised to the upside and this morning's data show the Fed's preferred measure of inflation, the PCE deflator, rose 0.3%, which was enough to drive the year-over-year rate lower to 2.5%," Wells Fargo's Tim Quinlan said.

"While risks today look skewed to the upside around inflation, the pricing environment remains highly uncertain. Consumers are price fatigued, and major retailers have acknowledged this. Tariffs are the most obvious threat to the pricing environment, and the last few tenths to the Fed's 2% target remain in the crosshair," Quinlan added.

Wall Street caught a bout of heavy selling in the final hour of trading on Thursday, as sentiment took a hit from a post-earnings fall in semiconductor giant Nvidia (NVDA) and more tariff-related moves by U.S. President Donald Trump.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Six Flags powers up for its first spring and summer as the dominant theme park player
data.description:
March is the traditional beginning of the theme park season. Six Flags Entertainment (SIX) heads into this year's spring and summer busy time confident that it will start to see tangible benefits from the mega-merger of legacy Six Flags and Cedar Fair that closed in July 2024. The company is also giving its theme parks a fresh look by introducing major new attractions at 11 of its 14 largest properties.

Morgan Stanley reiterated its Overwweight rating on Six Flags (SIX) after taking in the company's Q4 earnings report and conference call update. The bullish view on Six Flags Entertainment (NYSE:FUN) FUN is anchored in what MS sees as a compelling attendance-led growth opportunity. Analyst Thomas Yeh and his team raised estimates on FUN modestly following the company's robust 2025 adjusted EBITDA guidance. Notably, the firm expects double-digit adjusted EBITDA CAGR over the next three years.

Catalysts seen by Morgan Stanley for Six Flags (SIX) include the May 20 Investor Day event, non-core land monetization, and more synergy upside. Overall, 9 out of 11 sell-side firms covering Six Flags (SIX) have a Buy-equivalent rating.

On Seeking Alpha, Broyhill Asset Management issued a very bullish appraisal of SIX last month. "If management executes on its targets, we don’t think 10x is a stretch, particularly when one considers the added benefit of the recent C-Corp conversion, which should increase liquidity, drive institutional ownership, and expand the shareholder base," highlighted the firm.

Shares of Six Flags Entertainment (SIX) are down 8.7% on a year-to-date basis.

data.title: Moderna gets UK MHRA approval for its RSV Vaccine
data.description:
• Moderna (NASDAQ:MRNA) said on Friday that UK's Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency (MHRA) has granted marketing authorization for mRESVIA to prevent lower respiratory tract disease due to respiratory syncytial virus in adults 60 years of age and older.
  • The approval is based on positive data from its late stage trial conducted in about 37,000 adults of sixty years and older in 22 countries.
  • The stock is down 2.4% so far today.

data.title: Amneal projects $3B-$3.1B revenue in 2025 with biosimilars and CREXONT as key drivers
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Amneal Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:AMRX) Q4 2024

Management View

  • Chirag Patel, Co-CEO, highlighted Amneal's "stellar financial performance" in 2024, citing double-digit revenue and adjusted EBITDA growth, reduction of net leverage below four times, and notable contributions from all segments. Key strategic actions included the launch of CREXONT, entry into the GLP-1 market through a collaboration with Metsera, and expansion of the biosimilars pipeline.
  • CREXONT, a treatment for Parkinson's disease, achieved 1% market share within four months of launch and is on track to exceed 3% by year-end. The company anticipates U.S. peak sales of $300M to $500M for CREXONT.
  • CFO Tasos Konidaris stated, "In 2024, net leverage declined to 3.9 times, compared to 4.8 times at the end of 2023," supported by EBITDA growth and debt repayment. He added that Amneal expects continued top- and bottom-line growth in 2025, despite the loss of exclusivity for Rytary.

Outlook

  • Amneal projects 2025 total revenue of $3.0B-$3.1B, driven by double-digit growth in the Affordable Medicines segment, Specialty segment revenues of approximately $400M, and ongoing biosimilar expansion.
  • Adjusted EBITDA guidance for 2025 is set at $650M-$675M, reflecting 4%-8% growth. Adjusted EPS is expected between $0.65 and $0.70, aided by reduced interest expenses.
  • The company aims for 50% formulary coverage for CREXONT in 2025, with a long-term goal to match or surpass Rytary's 70% coverage.

Financial Results

  • Q4 revenues were $731M, representing 18% growth year-over-year, with all three segments contributing double-digit growth.
  • Biosimilar revenues reached $39M, a 49% increase from the prior year, while Specialty segment revenues grew 16% to $121M, driven by branded products like CREXONT.
  • Adjusted EBITDA for Q4 was $155M, reflecting consistent gross margins and investments in R&D and marketing. Adjusted EPS for the quarter was $0.12.
  • Full-year 2024 revenue was $2.8B, an increase of 17%, with adjusted gross margins at 42.4%. Full-year adjusted EBITDA was $627M, exceeding guidance.
  • Operating cash flow for 2024 was $348M, excluding one-time legal costs, surpassing original guidance.

Q&A

  • Chris Schott, J.P. Morgan, inquired about CREXONT's launch performance and formulary access. Chirag Patel emphasized that early uptake exceeded expectations, stating, "This already [has] 1,000-plus prescriptions per week just in four months." Joe Renda, Head of Specialty, added that current formulary coverage is about 30% and is expected to grow to 50% in 2025.
  • David Amsellem, Piper Sandler, questioned the financial implications of the Metsera collaboration and CapEx growth. CFO Konidaris explained that the GLP-1 initiative involves manageable CapEx of $100M-$150M over three years, partly offset by partner contributions.
  • Balaji Prasad, Barclays, asked about biosimilar strategy. Chirag Patel highlighted the potential for biosimilars as a $20B-$30B market, noting plans to expand capacity and pipeline with a focus on vertical integration.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed cautious optimism, focusing on CREXONT's performance, investment in biosimilars, and the Metsera collaboration. Questions reflected skepticism about long-term CapEx and competitive positioning in new markets.
  • Management displayed confidence, particularly in CREXONT's market potential and the biosimilar growth trajectory, using phrases like "we are very excited" and "this is a very large opportunity."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, the tone was more upbeat, supported by robust Q4 results and strategic progress.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance shifted positively, with 2025 revenue projected at $3.0B-$3.1B compared to previous expectations of $2.6B for 2024.
  • CREXONT's early success was emphasized more strongly, with management citing faster uptake compared to Rytary.
  • Biosimilar investments were highlighted as a key growth driver, whereas prior discussions focused more on the initial stages of the Metsera collaboration.
  • Analyst focus shifted toward competitive positioning and longer-term strategic implications of the GLP-1 initiative.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged challenges related to the Rytary loss of exclusivity in Q3 2025 but expressed confidence in offsetting it through CREXONT and biosimilar growth.
  • Analysts raised concerns about CapEx allocation for new facilities and the potential impact on debt reduction goals.
  • Market access and payer adoption for CREXONT remain critical for achieving projected sales.

Final Takeaway

Amneal Pharmaceuticals delivered strong Q4 and full-year 2024 results, driven by double-digit growth across all segments and strategic initiatives such as the CREXONT launch and biosimilar expansion. Looking ahead, the company projects 2025 revenue of $3.0B-$3.1B, supported by continued growth in Affordable Medicines, increased formulary coverage for CREXONT, and new biosimilar launches. While the Rytary loss of exclusivity presents a near-term challenge, management's confidence in offsetting impacts through diversified growth avenues remains clear. The Metsera collaboration in the GLP-1 market also positions Amneal for long-term growth, albeit with manageable CapEx commitments.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: U.S. consumers bracing for another year of rising beef prices
data.description:
As U.S. trade policy enacting tariffs of 10% to 25% on imports from China, Canada, and Mexico looms, Americans are bracing for higher prices for everything from lumber to avocados to beef.

Thanks to abundant pastureland, the U.S. has always provided the majority of its own beef. Imports from Mexico, Canada, and Brazil account for just 12% of the beef that is consumed by Americans. But since 2022, U.S. herds have been declining at an alarming rate as drought in the Great Plains has led to higher feed costs and diminished pastureland. This in turn drives the need for increased imports.

While the U.S. deals with the smallest cattle herds in more than 70 years, Canadian ranchers have also been faced with high feed costs and are culling their herds amid expectations for higher export costs, and the potential for decreased demand from U.S. consumers. Given the cycle of import/exports between the U.S. and Canada (Canada imports live animals from the U.S., puts them out to graze, slaughters them, and then sends the beef back to the U.S.), the dearth of cattle on both sides of the border spells even higher prices for consumers and commercial customers.

The U.S. can turn to Mexico to make up the difference, which already accounts for 22% of the beef imported into the U.S. But Mexican beef will also be subject to a 25% tariff beginning as early as next week.

The other option is importing meat from Brazil. However, Brazilian beef can also be hit with tariffs once a certain volume is reached. And with imports from Brazil already up 11% in 2024 from the year prior, that threshold could be reached this year.

Fortunately, 2024 saw a larger calf crop than expected along with the reintroduction of Mexican feeder cattle into the supply chain, both of which led the U.S. Department of Agriculture to raise its projected beef production in 2025 by 775M pounds. This, however, is still below supply/demand equilibrium and is expected to drive beef prices up another 3% this year.

Related Tickers: Conagra (NYSE:CAG), Hormel (HRL), Tyson Foods (TSN), JBS S.A. (OTCQX:JBSAY), B&G Foods (BGS), Sysco (SYY), US Foods (USFD), Performance Food Group (PFGC).

data.title: Bitcoin set to drop 11.7% W/W as selloff deepens
data.description:
Bitcoin (BTC-USD) is set to slump 11.7% on a weekly basis, as uncertainty fueled by looming tariffs has investors selling off risky assets.

The world’s largest digital currency started the week well above the $90K mark but briefly fell below the $80K on Friday.

“The crypto industry's close ties to the US equity market have led to a downward spiral of digital asset prices as investors sell off risk assets such as Bitcoin. Crypto prices are reaching past critical support levels that need to be regained if there are any hopes of avoiding a longer-term bear market,” said Ben El-Baz, Managing Director of HashKey Global.

In the month, BTC has dropped ~17% and is on course to record its worst month in three years. Earlier Friday, BTC risked a drop of ~23% when it fell below $79K.

It is down 27% from its all-time high that it touched on January 20—President Trump's inauguration.

Bitcoin’s (BTC-USD) downward spiral continued throughout the week, and fears of BTC entering a longer-term bear market has emerged.

The top cryptocurrency had been one of the most popular Trump trades, given expectations of a friendlier regulatory environment under the new administration. But risk appetite has been on the decline since Trump ramped up tariff threats for Canada, Mexico, China, and even the European Union.

“Crypto-friendly policies and frameworks will likely take some time before they materialize into tangible frameworks, while a fall in implied BTC volatility with falling prices is a negative sign that speculators have started to throw in the towel on higher prices in the near-term,” Chris Yu, Co-Founder and CEO of SignalPlus said.

Finally, expect continued scrutiny on MSTR as prices continue to trend lower given their recent buying splurge, so it's looking like a challenging period for crypto prices ahead before BTC can resume its path higher over the long term, Yu added.

The cryptocurrency’s struggle has led to investors to pull out more than $1B of spot bitcoin exchange-traded funds on Tuesday alone, marking the largest single-day cash exodus on record for the ETFs since their debut in January 2024.

If the risk-off sentiment continues, Bitcoin (BTC-USD) could lose all its gains from the post-election rally and outflows from crypto ETFs could swell further. The crypto fear and greed index, which tracks trader sentiment in the sector, is now flashing "Extreme Fear."

The cryptocurrency industry has also been reeling from the Bybit hack in the week.

Bybit said it successfully navigated the biggest cryptocurrency heist ever recorded, in which hackers stole about $1.5B in digital assets from the crypto exchange's Ether’s cold wallet.

More Notable News

  • The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission on Thursday dismissed its civil lawsuit against crypto exchange Coinbase Global (COIN) as the regulator pivots toward a softer stance on digital assets under the Trump administration.
  • Core Scientific (CORZ) announced on Wednesday an expansion of its relationship with CoreWeave, an AI Hyperscaler. The new agreement brings an additional $1.2B in contracted revenue across Core Scientific’s Denton TX location.
  • GameStop (GME) CEO Ryan Cohen confirmed on social media site X that he received a letter from Strive Asset Management urging the company to adopt bitcoin (BTC-USD) as a reserve asset.
  • Rezolve Ai (RZLV) is rolling out a $1B bitcoin (BTC-USD) treasury program, with an initial $100M investment, to directly support the commercialization of its to-be-launched AI-driven crypto payment platform, developed in collaboration with stablecoin issuer Tether.
  • Ken Griffin's Citadel Securities, the market-making powerhouse, is switching from a cautious stance to embracing crypto as President Donald Trump's administration adopts a more supportive approach to the digital asset class, according to a media report on Monday.
  • Robinhood Markets (HOOD) said the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission closed its investigation into Robinhood Crypto without taking any enforcement action.
  • Robinhood Market (HOOD) is leveraging a crypto-friendly Trump administration to expand its crypto offerings into areas like tokenization and other opportunities beyond traditional brokerage.
  • Strategy (MSTR) resumed its Bitcoin purchases and acquired 20.4K bitcoin (BTC-USD) in the week ended February 23.

Moves of other cryptocurrencies

Bitcoin (BTC-USD) down 0.4% to $85K as at 2:14 pm on Friday, Ether (ETH-USD) was down 4.1% to $4.4K, while DOGE (DOGE-USD) down 3.5%.

data.title: U.S. drilling rigs rise for fifth straight week, Baker Hughes says
data.description:
The number of active rigs drilling for oil and gas in the U.S. rose by 1 to 593 for a fifth straight weekly gain, Baker Hughes said Friday in its latest report.

Despite the increase, Baker Hughes said the total count was still down 36 rigs, or 6% below this time last year.

Drilling rigs targeting crude oil in the U.S. fell by 2 to 486, but natural gas rigs increased by 3 to 102 while 5 rigs remained classified as miscellaneous.

Rigs targeting oil in the Permian Basin added 1 to 302, the oil count in the Eagle Ford remained unchanged at 43, and the Williston Basin count also stayed flat at 32.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:USO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (UNG), (BOIL, (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG)

data.title: Google plans job cuts at HR, cloud units amid cost reduction moves
data.description:
Update: The story was updated with a statement from Google.

Alphabet's (NASDAQ:GOOG) (NASDAQ:GOOGL) Google informed employees in its “People Operations” and cloud organizations this week that it plans to cut jobs as part of internal reorganizations.

The tech giant will offer a voluntary exit program to U.S.-based, full-time employees in its human relations unit known as People Operations, beginning in early March.

“Our teams have continued to make changes to operate more efficiently, remove layers, and ensure they are set up for long term success. This work is ongoing as we continue to invest in our company's biggest priorities and the significant opportunities ahead," said Google spokesperson Brandon Asberry in an emailed statement to Seeking Alpha.

Google noted that the measures are not a company-wide layoff and that reorganizations are part of business. The company added that changes can be expected as teams continue to take steps to ensure they are focused on the right priorities.

On overseas roles at Google, the company noted that its largest employee presence is in the U.S. and that is not changing. The company added that its teams are continuing to position themselves to be more efficient, which for a small percentage, includes consolidating or broadening to other parts of the U.S. and to other countries.

Google added that it is continuing to hire for critical roles in the U.S. The impacted employees will all be able to apply for open roles across Google.

The company noted that is also helping impacted employees, in line with local requirements, to explore roles at Google and elsewhere, outplacement services, and severance offerings.

“To support our People Operations team’s innovative work ahead, we’re opening new roles and offering a voluntary exit program with severance for their US-based employees,” said Asberry.

Last month, it was reported that the company had offered the voluntary exit program to employees working in the Platforms & Devices unit.

The move comes on the heels of Alphabet CFO Anat Ashkenazi noting that one of her top priorities would be to carry out more cost-cutting as Google expands its spending on AI infrastructure in 2025, CNBC reported.

Under the exit program for HR employees, those who are level 4 and level 5 may receive a severance of 14 weeks of salary and one more week for every full year of service, the report added, citing a memo issued by HR chief Fiona Cicconi.

Separately, the company has laid off employees in several teams within its cloud unit, mainly impacting operations support staff. Some of the moves include moving roles to other countries, the report added, citing sources and different internal memos.

On Wednesday, Bloomberg News had reported the job cuts at Google’s cloud division.

The job cuts at the company’s cloud unit impacted the division's sales operations, customer experience, internal deal and go-to-market teams. Some affected employees’ roles are being relocated to India and Mexico City, the report added.

data.title: AstraZeneca's metastatic breast cancer treatment gets EU recommendation
data.description:
AstraZeneca (NASDAQ:AZN) and Daiichi Sankyo said on Friday that the European Medicines Agency (EMA) endorsed ENHERTU for the treatment of metastatic breast cancer.

Issuing a positive recommendation, EMA’s Committee for Medicinal Products for Human Use (CHMP) recommended the marketing authorization for ENHERTUas a monotherapy for the treatment of patients with unresectable or metastatic hormone receptor positive, HER2 low or HER2 ultralow breast cancer who have received at least one endocrine therapy.

ENHERTU is being jointly developed and commercialized by Daiichi Sankyo and AstraZeneca.
